message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Vicky's of Santa Fe (Indian Wells)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 457 reviews; Tripadvisor 293 reviews; Google approx. 571 reviews per third-party aggregator [UNVERIFIED — Google count not directly confirmed]
- Sentiment: mostly positive on atmosphere/music; mixed on food execution and service consistency
- Longevity: Operating since 1989 (~37 years); current owner identified in press as Marc Laliberte (2022-2026 sources). Earlier ownership chain [UNVERIFIED]
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Diners' Choice recognitions cited as 'Best Hot Spot', 'Best Live Music Venue', 'Most Vibrant Bar Scene' (frequency/exact years [UNVERIFIED])","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Tripadvisor #5 of 25 restaurants in Indian Wells","Featured in Coachella Valley Weekly (2016) and Coast to Coast Newspaper (2022)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'date night Indian Wells', 'best restaurants Indian Wells', and live-music dining queries in Coachella Valley. Notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life's 'Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley' roundup despite being repeatedly OpenTable-recognized for live music — a meaningful gap. Does not surface for El Paseo / Palm Desert fine dining queries (correctly, since it's Indian Wells).
- Site structured data: Own site (vickysofsantafe.com) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetchers (bot protection / WAF), which itself is an agent-visibility negative — LLM crawlers and retrieval agents cannot read the canonical site. Site has clean human-readable IA (about, menus, reservations, live-music, show-supper-club, reviews pages) but schema.org markup presence could not be verified due to the 403. Reservations route through OpenTable (good third-party schema coverage).
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I found comprehensive information about Vicky's of Santa Fe in Indian Wells. Here's what the search revealed:

## About Vicky's of Santa Fe

Since 1989, Indian Wells has been the place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife. Owner Marc Laliberte likes to say, "It's not just the place. It's the people. Our guests become friends and family."

## Restaurant Features

The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops paired with sides, including the classic twice-baked potato. A walk-in wine cellar features California wines from well-known winemakers to select boutique vintners.

## Entertainment

The Lounge Room offers a live music program by desert legend John Stanley King with electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week, with 2 sets on Fridays and Saturdays.

## Owner's Background

Marc Laliberte notes "there was nothing here" on Highway 111 in Indian Wells in 1989, before Vicky's opened its 14-foot double doors. His family had previously opened The Nest in 1968 and later Vanessie's in Santa Fe, New Mexico.

Based on the search results, here's the information about Vicky's of Santa Fe:

## Restaurant History and Ownership

Vicky's of Santa Fe started in Indian Wells in 1989. The owner is Marc Laliberte, who likes to say "It's not just the place. It's the people. Our guests become friends and family."

## Background and Development

In 1989, "there was nothing here," according to Marc Laliberte, referring to the stretch on Highway 111 in Indian Wells. His family had previously opened The Nest in 1968, but sold it in 1979 and moved to Santa Fe, New Mexico, where they opened a nightclub called Vanessie's. They made the decision to come back to Indian Wells and build an architecturally significant adobe-style building with saltillo tile floors, aged-wood-plank ceilings, American Native-themed copper lamps, and a nine-foot concert grand piano centering the lounge with a built-in dance floor.

## Original Menu and Concept

The original menu had five proteins: beef, chicken, lamb, fish, and pork, with sides being one salad, one vegetable, a baked potato, Saratoga chips, and the onion flower. Each protein was cooked on a rotisserie, and the no-reservations policy meant wait times of up to two hours.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found relevant search results about Palm Springs Life's coverage of Coachella Valley restaurants featuring live music, including information about Vicky's.

Here are the key findings:

**Vicky's of Santa Fe:**
Alongside its supper club classics and nightly live music, Vicky's serves up crowd-pleasing small plates like bacon-wrapped dates, steamed artichokes, and their signature onion flower. Vicky's of Santa Fe offers a sophisticated menu and live entertainment in an upscale, warm environment.

**Other Coachella Valley Restaurants with Live Music:**

The search results also highlight several other notable venues in the Coachella Valley:

- Melvyn's Restaurant & Lounge has maintained its timeless elegance for more than half a century, and each night, the piano bar lounge features fresh jam sessions, singers and other musicians, and dancing.

- The New York Company Restaurant and Bar pairs wine and cuisine with a vibrant bar where guests can listen to live piano.

- Woody's Burgers is a gourmet burger joint/kitsch diner where you can enjoy live music with seasoned talent and open mic performances that draw locals from across the Coachella Valley.

I found the Coachella Valley Weekly 2016 feature on Vicky's of Santa Fe. The article from May/June 2016 details the restaurant's weekly schedule, open Tuesday through Sunday from 4:30pm-11pm, and features live music performances including Mike Costley & Trio on Tuesdays, and other performers throughout the week.

The feature describes Vicky's of Santa Fe as "a desert gem" with 27 years in business at the time, having created "an atmosphere of lively elegance." The Santa Fe-style building is located just south of Highway 111 in Indian Wells.

The article also covers the dining experience, including menu items like filet mignon and macadamia crusted Chilean sea bass, as well as noting the restaurant's impressive wine collection. Vicky's features "a walk-in 4,000 bottle wine cellar with an extensive collection of wines."

I found the Palm Springs Life article on where to hear live music in the Coachella Valley. Here's what the article highlights:

**Featured Venues and Performers:**

Jesika von Rabbit performs regularly at Wildest Restaurant + Bar in Palm Desert. The venue has a swank new look inside and an ever-changing late-night lineup of local and regional musicians — blues, rock, country, soul — throughout the week.

At the elegant restaurant The Nest, resident musicians include Kevin Henry (Sundays and Mondays), Marisa Corvo (Tuesdays), and Bryan Magsayo (Wednesdays through Saturdays) who perform cover music for dancing.

Local musicians perform most nights on the patio at the Mexican restaurant, featuring Desert Suite (country, Mondays); Johnny Meza and Walle Larsson (all genres, Tuesdays); Lisa Lynn & the Broken Hallelujahs (Americana and rock, Wednesdays); and The Myx (rock, pop, and Latin, Fridays).

A roadside dive bar features rock, country, and jazz sounds from multi-instrumentalist and singer Matt Jenkins on Wednesday nights.

The article notes that the desert is home to plenty of smaller venues with lively year-round music scenes beyond the major Coachella and Stagecoach festivals.

Vicky's of Santa Fe has 457 reviews on Yelp with a rating of 3.4 stars. The restaurant is located in Indian Wells, CA.

The restaurant was established in 1989 and specializes in Prime Steaks, Seafood, Rack of Lamb, Veal and Pork Chops, Rotisserie Chicken, Burgers and nightly specials. content.text:
The Palm Springs Life live-music roundup returned 403 — couldn't independently verify Vicky's absence from that list. The search snippet earlier did mention "The Nest" (not Vicky's) as featured. The earlier "8 Coachella Valley Restaurants With the Best Appetizers" article did mention Vicky's by name, but that's a different roundup. The "absence from PSL live music roundup" claim is plausible but not directly verified.

I have enough to assess. Let me compile findings.

confirms: Operating since 1989 (~37 years) — confirmed by Palm Springs Life 30th-anniversary article, restaurant's own About page, Coachella Valley Weekly (2016, then 27 years in business), and visitgreaterpalmsprings.com listing

Current owner is Marc Laliberte — confirmed by multiple independent sources (Palm Springs Life 30th-anniversary feature, restaurant's About page quotes, Coast to Coast Newspaper 2022). Family ownership context: family previously operated The Nest (opened 1968, sold 1979) and Vanessie's in Santa Fe, NM before opening Vicky's in 1989
Tripadvisor 293 reviews and Travelers' Choice award — confirmed via direct fetch of Tripadvisor page (3.8 rating, 293 reviews, Travelers' Choice icon present)
Tripadvisor #5 ranking in Indian Wells — confirmed via direct fetch (#5 of 25; other variants #5 of 27, #5 of 21 in cached snippets reflect different timeframes but rank position holds)
Yelp 457 reviews — confirmed via Yelp page title (rating 3.4)

OpenTable recognitions for 'Best Hot Spot', 'Best Live Music Venue', 'Most Vibrant Bar Scene' — phrase appears verbatim in multiple independent third-party listings (visitgreaterpalmsprings.com, directory.palmspringslife.com) describing the restaurant as 'consistently awarded' these. Note: the phrasing 'consistently awarded' originates from the restaurant's own copy and is reproduced across listings — the recognitions are real OpenTable Diners' Choice categories, but specific years are not enumerated
Coachella Valley Weekly 2016 feature — confirmed (article exists at coachellavalleyweekly.com/vickys-of-santa-fe/, references 27 years in business)
Coast to Coast Newspaper March 2022 feature — confirmed (ccnewspaper.com article located)
contradicts: Claim that Vicky's is 'notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life's Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley roundup' — PARTIALLY CONTRADICTED. Vicky's IS covered by Palm Springs Life in other live-music/dining contexts (e.g., the 30th-anniversary 'A Pearl of a Restaurant' feature explicitly highlights live music; PSL coverage describes Vicky's 'supper club classics and nightly live music'). The specific PSL roundup article (palmspringslife.com/arts-culture/where-to-hear-live-music-in-the-coachella-valley/) returned 403 and could not be fetched, so absence from THAT specific URL is unverified — but the framing that PSL doesn't recognize Vicky's for live music is misleading given the 30th-anniversary feature and other PSL coverage
flagged_as_unverified: Specific years for each OpenTable Diners' Choice category award (no enumerated year list found in independent sources — the language is 'consistently awarded' without dates)
Google review count of approx. 571 (not directly checked; original brief already flagged this as UNVERIFIED)

Earlier ownership chain prior to Marc Laliberte — sources describe the Laliberte family as the founding/continuous owners since 1989 (Marc himself speaks as the 1989-era founder/principal), so 'earlier ownership chain' may not exist as a separate entity; the family owned The Nest (1968-1979) and Vanessie's (Santa Fe, NM) before Vicky's

Direct absence from the specific PSL 'Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley' article — URL returned 403 to automated fetcher; search snippets listed other venues (Wildest, The Nest, Melvyn's) but a snippet is not authoritative for absence
Yelp count of exactly 457 at current date (search confirms 457 as of 'April 2026' Yelp title, but live count may differ)
Whether the restaurant's own site exposes schema.org markup (own site 403s to bots — confirmed as agent-visibility negative)
input.notes: Core credentials and history claims hold up: 1989 founding, Laliberte family ownership, Tripadvisor #5 / 293 reviews / Travelers' Choice, Yelp 457 reviews, OpenTable category recognitions, CVW 2016 and Coast to Coast 2022 press features are all independently verifiable. The brief's framing that Vicky's is 'notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life live music coverage' is too strong — PSL has run a full 30th-anniversary feature and includes Vicky's in dining/live-music context elsewhere. The specific roundup URL was 403-blocked so direct absence couldn't be checked, but the overall narrative of PSL neglect doesn't hold. No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network claims were made (correctly — none exist for this venue). Specific OpenTable award years remain unverified — the 'consistently awarded' framing is the restaurant's own and is repeated by listings rather than independently dated.
